problem: Mode 3 Step 1b pattern #2 ("stale complete expansions") is written as `status==='complete' && !integrated && !routed_to_curmudgeon`. This flags any expansion that is intentionally held by the decider via `integration_blocked_until`. Today's Mode 3 run hit 7 such items (EXP-207, 208, 209, 210, 212, 213 — all Phase A design-refresh items gated on operator HNOTE approval) and would have re-escalated them as 're-work' waste. They are NOT re-work; they are legitimately waiting for external gating. The filter must exclude `integration_blocked_until`-holding items, and should also exclude items that have been superseded/reallocated (EXP-211 was reallocated to EXP-214 but the tracker still shows it as stranded).

proposed_fix.summary: Tighten the Step 1b pattern #2 `node -e` snippet in `tinker-cost-engineering.md` to exclude items whose `integration_blocked_until` field is truthy, and to surface-but-separately-classify `superseded_by`/`reallocated_to` items. The change is a single-file edit to a reference markdown; the logic change is additive (more specific, not less).

files_to_modify.explanation: Adds two exclusions to the re-work filter (integration_blocked_until, superseded_by/reallocated_to) and a separate HELD bucket so the tinker can still see them but not classify them as waste. Reads `fs` explicitly (the original snippet omitted the require; it happens to work because the surrounding block adds it, but the standalone version fails without it — harmless hardening).

tradeoffs: Gain: Mode 3 stops generating false-positive 'stale complete' findings when the operator has open design-refresh holds. Tinker runs produce more-signal/less-noise reports. Loss: if an item ever gets `integration_blocked_until` set and then the gate is forever-absent (operator never posts the HNOTE), the bug becomes invisible to the stale-complete check. Mitigation: the HELD bucket still surfaces them for situational awareness, and a separate future check (not in this PROP) can alert if HELD age exceeds N days.
estimated_impact: Today's Mode 3 run would have flagged 6 false positives (EXP-207,208,209,210,212,213). Applied, the filter returns 1 true positive (EXP-211 — tracker-hygiene item, not re-work but a genuine stale-complete). Across all future Mode 3 runs while Phase A design-refresh is open, this eliminates ~5-10 false findings per run, meaning the remaining findings are actually actionable.
verification_pattern: grep -q 'integration_blocked_until' monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-cost-engineering.md && grep -q 'HELD (awaiting operator gate' monitor/prompts/reference/tinker-cost-engineering.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN

counter_arguments_addressed.claim: If a `integration_blocked_until` gate is forever-open, the item stays stranded and tinker no longer catches it.
counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The HELD bucket surfaces them separately; tinker still reports their existence, just not as re-work waste. A future Mode 3 enhancement can add an age threshold to HELD (alert if held >7 days). Within Phase A (the current design refresh), 7 days of held items is exactly what we'd expect.

counter_arguments_addressed.claim: This is a prompt-diet change that tinker should not self-apply.
counter_arguments_addressed.counter: The change is mechanical (regex-level edit to a filter predicate), the target is a reference module that tinker itself reads, and the verification pattern is a simple grep. Mode 4 Step 1 explicitly permits self-fixes on tinker's own reference material. The PROP route is still used here for transparency — so the fix lands in a review-able patch rather than a silent edit.
